content:
It is a matter of considerable pleasure and privilege for me to have received an invitation for membership of an International Network of Law and Culture, from the founder Professor Antonio Albanese, a respected Professor of Private Law at the University of Bologna. Titled 'Falling in Law' (https://lnkd.in/dAz2PrEH), this network is an international initiative that aims to explore and link law with various artistic and cultural expressions, with the goals of creating a space where law transcends traditional limits, highlighting its humanistic matrix, promoting the exchange of ideas between jurists and scholars of other disciplines, and placing deserving students at the center of learning and innovation. 

This invitation has been extended to me in connection with my previous work in the domain of law, literature, and popular culture, including in particular the two courses curated by myself for the undergraduate programme offered at my university. The first one was titled 'An Interface between Law and Fantasy Fiction Literature: Special Focus on Rowling's Potterverse', and it had been recognised as the first course of its kind in the world by almost all the leading global media including BBC, the Guardian and others. The second one was titled 'Comics, Graphic Novels, and the Law', and that too had been recognised as the first of its kind in India. Following these courses, I had the honour of one of my related publications having been accepted as a book chapter titled 'Using Contemporary Literature and Popular Culture to Study Law: The Possibilities Inherent in Fantasy Fiction and Harry Potter' for the book 'Indian Yearbook of Law and Interdisciplinary Studies: Pluralistic Discourse', published by Routledge alongside contributions by legal luminaries like Professor Baxi himself.

Hopefully I will be able to learn from the global domain experts who are part of this network and from the contributions made by them in law, literature, culture, and legal education.
